H202.derivation: probably from the same as H205 (אָוֶן) (in the sense of effort, but successful);

H205.derivation: from an unused root perhaps meaning properly, to pant (hence, to exert oneself, usually in vain; to come to naught);
H205.strongs_def: strictly nothingness; also trouble. vanity, wickedness; specifically an idol
H205.kjv_def: affliction, evil, false, idol, iniquity, mischief, mourners(-ing), naught, sorrow, unjust, unrighteous, vain, vanity, wicked(-ness). Compare H369 (אַיִן).
H205.usage: iniquity(47x), vanity(6x), affliction(3x), mischief(3x), unrighteous(2x), evil(1x), false(1x), idol(1x), mourners(1x), mourning(1x), nought(1x), sorrow(1x), unjust(1x), vain(1x)

H206.strongs_def: Aven, the contemptuous synonym of three places, one in Coele-Syria, one in Egypt (On), and one in Palestine (Bethel)
H206.kjv_def: Aven. See also H204 (אוֹן), H1007 (בֵּית אָוֶן).

H207.derivation: or (shortened) אֹנוֹ; prolonged from H202 (אוֹן); strong;

H208.strongs_def: Onam, the name of an Edomite and of an Israelite

H211.derivation: or (shortened) אֹפִיר; and אוֹפִר; of uncertain derivation;
H211.strongs_def: Ophir, the name of a son of Joktan, and of a gold region in the East
H211.pos: proper locative noun, proper masculine noun

H212.derivation: or (shortened) אֹפָן; from an unused root meaning to revolve;

H213.strongs_def: to press; (by implication) to be close, hurry, withdraw

H215.strongs_def: to be (causative, make) luminous (literally and metaphorically)
H215.kjv_def: [idiom] break of day, glorious, kindle, (be, en-, give, show) light (-en, -ened), set on fire, shine.
H215.usage: light(19x), shine(14x), enlighten(5x), fire(1x), give(1x), glorious(1x), kindle(1x)

H216.strongs_def: illumination or (concrete) luminary (in every sense, including lightning, happiness, etc.)
H216.kjv_def: bright, clear, [phrase] day, light (-ning), morning, sun.
H216.usage: day(2x), bright(1x), clear(1x), flood(1x), herbs(1x), lightning(1x), morning(1x), sun(1x)

H217.strongs_def: flame; hence (in the plural) the East (as being the region of light)

H218.strongs_def: Ur, a place in Chaldaea; also an Israelite

H219.strongs_def: luminousness, i.e. (figuratively) prosperity; also a plant (as being bright)

H221.strongs_def: Uri, the name of three Israelites

H222.derivation: from H217 (אוּר) and H410 (אֵל); flame of God;
H222.strongs_def: Uriel, the name of two Israelites

H223.derivation: or (prolonged) אוּרִיָּהוּ ; from H217 (אוּר) and H3050 (יָהּ); flame of Jah;
H223.strongs_def: Urijah, the name of one Hittite and five Israelites

H224.strongs_def: Urim, the oracular brilliancy of the figures in the high-priest's breastplate

H225.strongs_def: properly, to come, i.e. (implied) to assent

H226.derivation: probably from H225 (אוּת) (in the sense of appearing);
H226.strongs_def: a signal (literally or figuratively), as aflag, beacon, monument, omen, prodigy, evidence, etc.

H227.strongs_def: at that time or place; also as a conjunction, therefore
H227.kjv_def: beginning, for, from, hitherto, now, of old, once, since, then, at which time, yet.

H232.strongs_def: something girt; a belt, also a band

H235.strongs_def: to go away, hence, to disappear
H235.kjv_def: fail, gad about, go to and fro (but in Ezekiel 27:19 the word is rendered by many 'from Uzal,' by others 'yarn'), be gone (spent).

H237.strongs_def: Ezel, a memorial stone in Palestine

H238.derivation: a primitive root; probably to expand; but used only as a denominative from H241 (אֹזֶן);
H238.strongs_def: to broaden out the ear (with the hand), i.e. (by implication) to listen
H238.kjv_def: give (perceive by the) ear, hear(-ken). See H239 (אָזַן).

H239.derivation: a primitive root (rather identical with H238 (אָזַן) through the idea of scales as if two ears);

H240.strongs_def: a spade or paddle (as having a broad end)

H241.strongs_def: broadness. i.e. (concrete) the ear (from its form in man)
H241.kjv_def: [phrase] advertise, audience, [phrase] displease, ear, hearing, [phrase] show.

H242.derivation: from H238 (אָזַן) and H7609 (שֶׁאֱרָה); plot of Sheerah (i.e. settled by him);

H243.derivation: from H238 (אָזַן) and H8396 (תָּבוֹר); flats (i.e. tops) of Tabor (i.e. situated on it);

H244.strongs_def: Ozni, an Israelite; also an Oznite (collectively), his descendant

H245.derivation: from H238 (אָזַן) and H3050 (יָהּ); heard by Jah;

H249.derivation: from H2224 (זָרַח) (in the sense of springing up);
H249.strongs_def: a spontaneous growth, i.e. native (tree or persons)
H249.kjv_def: bay tree, (home-) born (in the land), of the (one's own) country (nation).

H250.strongs_def: an Ezrachite or descendant of Zerach

H251.strongs_def: a brother (used in the widest sense of literal relationship and metaphorical affinity or resemblance (like father))
H251.kjv_def: another, brother(-ly); kindred, like, other. Compare also the proper names beginning with 'Ah-' or 'Ahi-'.
H251.usage: brethren(332x), brother(269x), another(23x), brotherly(1x), kindred(1x), like(1x), another(1x), other(1x)

H252.strongs_def: {a brother (used in the widest sense of literal relationship and metaphorical affinity or resemblance)}

H255.strongs_def: a howler or lonesome wild animal

H256.derivation: once (by contraction) אֶחָב (Jeremiah 29:22); from H251 (אָח) and H1 (אָב); brother (i.e. friend) of (his) father;
H256.strongs_def: Achab, the name of a king of Israel and of a prophet at Babylon

H257.derivation: from H251 (אָח) and H995 (בִּין); brother (i.e. possessor) of understanding;

H259.strongs_def: properly, united, i.e. one; or (as an ordinal) first
H259.kjv_def: a, alike, alone, altogether, and, any(-thing), apiece, a certain, (dai-) ly, each (one), [phrase] eleven, every, few, first, [phrase] highway, a man, once, one, only, other, some, together,
H259.usage: one(687x), first(36x), another(35x), other(30x), any(18x), once(13x), every(10x), certain(9x), an(7x), some(7x)

H260.strongs_def: a bulrush or any marshy grass (particularly that along the Nile)

H261.strongs_def: Echud, the name of three Israelites

H262.derivation: from H2331 (חָוָה) (in the sense of H2324 (חֲוָא));

H266.strongs_def: an Achochite or descendant of Achoach

H267.derivation: perhaps from H251 (אָח) and H4325 (מַיִם); brother (i.e. neighbour) of water;

H268.strongs_def: the hinder part; hence (adverb) behind, backward; also (as facing north) the West
H268.kjv_def: after(-ward), back (part, -side, -ward), hereafter, (be-) hind(-er part), time to come, without.
H268.usage: backward(11x), behind(5x), afterwards(1x), backside(1x), hereafter(1x), without(1x)

H269.strongs_def: a sister (used very widely (like brother), literally and figuratively)

H270.strongs_def: to seize (often with the accessory idea of holding in possession)
H270.kjv_def: [phrase] be affrighted, bar, (catch, lay, take) hold (back), come upon, fasten, handle, portion, (get, have or take) possess(-ion).

H271.strongs_def: Achaz, the name of a Jewish king and of an Israelite

H272.strongs_def: something seized, i.e. a possession (especially of land)

H274.derivation: or (prolonged) אֲחַזְיָהוּ ; from H270 (אָחַז) and H3050 (יָהּ); Jah has seized;
H274.strongs_def: Achazjah, the name of a Jewish and an Israelite king

H277.strongs_def: Achi, the name of two Israelites

H279.derivation: from H251 (אָח) and H517 (אֵם); brother of the mother (i.e. uncle);

H281.derivation: or (prolonged) אֲחִיָּהוּ ; from H251 (אָח) and H3050 (יָהּ); brother (i.e. worshipper) of Jah;
H281.strongs_def: Achijah, the name of nine Israelites

H282.derivation: from H251 (אָח) and H1935 (הוֹד); brother (i.e. possessor) of renown;

H283.strongs_def: Achio, the name of three Israelites

H284.derivation: from H251 (אָח) and H2330 (חוּד); brother of a riddle (i.e. mysterious);

H285.derivation: from H251 (אָח) and H2898 (טוּב); brother of goodness;
H285.strongs_def: Achitub, the name of several priests

H286.derivation: from H251 (אָח) and H3205 (יָלַד); brother of one born;

H287.derivation: from H251 (אָח) and H4191 (מוּת); brother of death;

H288.derivation: from H251 (אָח) and H4428 (מֶלֶךְ); brother of (the) king;
H288.strongs_def: Achimelek, the name of an Israelite and of a Hittite

H289.derivation: or אֲחִימָן ; from H251 (אָח) and H4480 (מִן); brother of a portion (i.e. gift);
H289.strongs_def: Achiman, the name of an Anakite and of an Israelite

H290.derivation: from H251 (אָח) and the equivalent of H4619 (מַעַץ); brother of anger;
H290.strongs_def: Achimaats, the name of three Israelites

H292.derivation: from H251 (אָח) and H5068 (נָדַב); brother of liberality;

H293.derivation: from H251 (אָח) and H5278 (נֹעַם); brother of pleasantness;
H293.strongs_def: Achinoam, the name of two Israelitesses

H294.derivation: from H251 (אָח) and H5564 (סָמַךְ); brother of support;

H295.derivation: from H251 (אָח) and H5828 (עֵזֶר); brother of help;
H295.strongs_def: Achiezer, the name of two Israelites

H296.derivation: from H251 (אָח) and H6965 (קוּם); brother of rising (i.e. high);

H297.derivation: from H251 (אָח) and H7311 (רוּם); brother of height (i.e. high);

H298.strongs_def: an Achiramite or descendant (collectively) of Achiram

H299.derivation: from H251 (אָח) and H7451 (רַע); brother of wrong;
